D10 Imaging L3 Apply Reference

Blue → Orthochromatic → Panchromatic

three eras of B&W film: each step extended spectral sensitivity into a longer range — and changed how red, green, blue scenes render as grey

Pre-1873 silver halide was only sensitive to blue and UV — red and green objects rendered black or dark grey. Vogel's 1873 dye-sensitization extended response into green (orthochromatic); 1906 Wratten introduced red sensitization (panchromatic). A panchromatic emulsion is what every modern B&W film is.

A colour scene is rendered three ways. Toggle the era and watch the same patches change grey value — reds turn black under blue-sensitive, mid-grey under panchromatic; greens dark under blue-sensitive, mid-grey under ortho/pan.
